title: Muelleranthus stipularis
intro: Muelleranthus stipularis, commonly known as the Stipular Flower or Mueller's Flower, is a flowering plant native to Australia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Muelleranthus stipularis include flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Muelleranthus stipularis include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to soothe respiratory issues and promote overall well-being.
